meta.title: Streaming reconciliation + Streaming example page + example-payload seed shelf
meta.author: planner (design seat)

nodes.label: Streaming SPEC/LLD v0.2 reconciliation (done in this intake)
nodes.accept: a2ui-streaming-pipeline.{spec,lld}.md at v0.2: zero `renderer heal.ts` citations remain (grep); the LLD component map carries a State column with all C1-C7 unbuilt + the realized-in-renderer boundary note; SPEC-R1 carries the healing-ownership sentence; all cross-references resolve

nodes.label: ADR-0055 (example seed shelf) PROPOSED → ratified
nodes.accept: adr_check.py exits 0; Status stays `proposed` until Kim rules forks F1/F2; build slices dispatch only after acceptance

nodes.label: seed modules + ExampleSeed type + the "./examples" subpath export
nodes.accept: src/examples/{types,canvas-button,dynamic-lists,generative-form,patterns,index}.ts exist; 11 seeds exported (1 canvas + 4 lists + 1 form + 5 patterns); package.json exports gains "./examples" and the ROOT barrel does not re-export them (tree-shake: a root-barrel consumer bundles zero examples bytes); the form seed is the re-sliced fine-grained stream

nodes.label: the standing validity gate (src/examples/examples.test.ts)
nodes.accept: npm test runs it with zero vitest-config change: per seed, validateA2ui verdict valid AND a jsdom real-host smoke (mount → ingest each message as a JSONL line → finalize) emits zero error messages; a deliberately-broken local fixture (negative control, not exported) fails the gate

nodes.label: validate.ts callFunction-envelope widening (the MESSAGE_KINDS parity gap)
nodes.accept: validateA2ui accepts a spec-legal callFunction envelope (functionCallId string + callFunction.call string, no surfaceId) and still rejects a malformed one as SCHEMA; existing validate.test.ts stays green; dispatch/validator envelope sets are asserted equal by a parity probe

nodes.label: page migration (canvas · list · form · patterns import the seeds)
nodes.accept: all four pages import @agent-ui/a2ui/examples and feed the SAME seed objects they display; no payload const remains in site/pages/*.ts except the canvas RPC literals; check:site green; pages behavior-identical in a hand browser pass

nodes.label: Streaming example page (site/a2ui-stream.html + pages/a2ui-stream.{ts,css})
nodes.accept: check green; demo 1 streams the SHARED generative-form seed line-by-line (payload pane grows with the feed, first-paint metric line k/N shown); demo 2 feeds a root-last PERMUTATION of the same seed objects (blank until root, then full paint); the labeled fault-injection affordance emits PARSE + the stream continues; Replay + Step controls, auto-run once, no loops; finalize fires at stream end with a clean log

nodes.label: site TOC entries (pages/_page.ts NAV + main.ts CARD_GROUPS)
nodes.accept: both renderings list the streaming page in the A2UI group; site-toc gate green; no dead hrefs

nodes.label: wave integration gate
nodes.accept: npm run check && npm test exit 0 at the integration commit; the examples gate is part of the run; no components-package source touched

actions.label: reconcile the streaming docs to the realized tree (honest realized/unrealized boundary)
pin the seed-shelf home + gate as a ratifiable decision
own every example payload once, package-side, corpus-aligned
prove every seed valid + renderable at check time (close the runtime-only gap)
teach the shared validator every envelope the dispatcher routes
derive the four pages from the seeds (shown ≡ fed ≡ gated)
stream one payload incrementally with replay/step pacing
prove ordering tolerance and fault isolation live
surface progressive first paint as a visible metric
navigate to the streaming page from both TOC renderings
hold the wave green across type + behavior gates

edges.why: the seed shape, subpath policy, and RPC-stays-page-local rule are ADR-0055's ratified contract — the modules encode it
the gate iterates the exported seed set; it cannot exist before the seeds it validates
the pages import the seed modules — migration needs the exports to exist
the streaming page imports the SAME generative-form seed (single-owner reuse is the point)
check:site covers the migrated page modules at the integration commit
the integration gate includes the new examples test in npm test
TOC links must point at an existing page (dead-href guard)
the site-toc gate runs green only after both renderings are edited
